decision: Use the Notion database 'Story Analysis & Reading Tracker' as the system of record for reading status and story-analysis metadata.
context: The handoff requested maintenance and expansion of the Notion tracker for both personal reading and future Assistant Scenario Adapter / Story Analysis Tool use.
outcome: Updated the live Notion database directly instead of creating a parallel local tracker.

decision: Expand 'Story Tool Tags' rather than adding separate category fields for the new analysis categories.
context: The tracker already had a multi-select 'Story Tool Tags' property for analysis labels such as Reader Trust, Rule Drift, and Worldbuilding.
outcome: Added Genre Saturation, Emotional Repetition, Narrative Weight Mismatch, and Emotional Continuity Violation as multi-select options.

decision: Create missing story rows when exact-title Notion searches do not find an existing tracker entry.
context: The handoff referenced S-Classes That I Raised, Patron of Villains, and The Regressed Demon Lord Is Kind, but exact tracker searches did not return matching rows.
outcome: Created new tracker rows for those titles instead of overloading unrelated search results.

decision: Scope the new app as a fully Convex-backed private reading tracker and recommendation system.
context: The product direction was reduced from chat/MCP/public corpus to an Overseerr/MyAnimeList-inspired tracker with title search, reading status, reviews, background enrichment, and scheduled LLM analysis.
outcome: Convex will own auth, database, API functions, scheduled jobs, source-cache records, and external OpenRouter/source calls; the app remains open source while deployment data stays private.

decision: Treat official platform links as first-class source records rather than ingesting images or chapter text.
context: The app should link back to rights-holder platforms such as Tapas for The Beginning After the End and avoid licensed-content scraping.
outcome: Backlog will prioritize platformLink/sourceRecord provenance, confidence, cache state, and refresh jobs over content ingestion.

decision: Use GitHub Issues and GitHub Projects as the initial backlog system for the open-source repository.
context: The user asked to create a GitHub repository with the gh CLI and start a backlog using GitHub Projects.
outcome: Create a public GitHub repository, issue labels, milestone, backlog issues, and a GitHub Project linked to the repository when gh has the required project scope.

decision: Create the public GitHub repository and public GitHub Project for the initial backlog.
context: The project is open-source code with private deployment data; GitHub Issues and the GitHub Project are public planning artifacts while deployment data remains private.
outcome: Created https://github.com/alexcatdad/manhwa-recs and GitHub Project https://github.com/users/alexcatdad/projects/1 with 12 MVP backlog issues linked to the project.
